proposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Lower Extremity Trauma Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Composite pelvis-to-foot model linking pelvic ring, knee, leg, and hindfoot structures for trauma neighborhoods.
Bony pelvic ring formed by ilium, ischium, pubis, and sacrum.
Posterior pelvic anchor articulating with ilia at the sacroiliac joints.
Posterior pelvic articulation between sacrum and ilium; key pelvic ring stability structure.
Anterior midline pelvic articulation; disruption indicates pelvic ring instability.
Lateral pelvic ilium region relevant to acetabular column and pelvic ring injuries.
Hip socket formed by ilium, ischium, and pubis; injured in acetabular fractures.
Femoral shaft between proximal and distal metaphyseal regions.
Distal femoral metaphysis and condylar region forming the proximal knee.
Proximal tibial articular surface bearing medial and lateral condylar load.
Sesamoid bone within the extensor mechanism at the anterior knee.
Quadriceps-patella-tibial tubercle linkage enabling knee extension.
Primary knee stabilizer commonly injured with tibial plateau and knee trauma.
Posterior knee stabilizer relevant in high-energy knee and plateau injuries.
Major knee-region artery at risk in tibial plateau, distal femur, and knee dislocation trauma.
Lateral knee nerve at risk in tibial plateau and proximal fibular injuries.
Tibiotalar and subtalar articular bone with limited vascular supply.
Hindfoot bone articulating with talus at the subtalar joint.
Talocalcaneal articulation governing hindfoot alignment after calcaneus and talus injuries.
Tarsometatarsal articulation complex; instability defines Lisfranc injury.
Central foot region including navicular, cuneiforms, and cuboid linking hindfoot to forefoot.
Plantar fascia and soft-tissue envelope relevant to calcaneus fracture swelling and wound risk.
Supracondylar femur fracture with articular involvement and vascular proximity concerns.
Open reduction internal fixation of displaced distal femur fracture.

proposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Intra-Articular Distal Femur Fracture

metadata.description: Distal femur fracture with condylar articular step-off or comminution.
Angular or rotational malalignment after distal femur fracture healing.

relationship_metadata.note: patient factors and soft-tissue status dependent

metadata.claim_text: Distal femur fractures require assessment of articular involvement, coronal sagittal alignment, and popliteal artery proximity.
High-energy distal femur fractures can injure the popliteal artery despite a warm foot and palpable dorsalis pedis pulse.
Managing displaced intra-articular distal femur fractures nonoperatively without articular reduction discussion.
Describe condylar involvement, vascular exam, soft-tissue condition, fixation construct plan, and malunion prevention goals.
Distal femur articular surface congruity with the tibial plateau determines knee joint loading after fracture healing.

metadata.trigger: Intra-articular distal femur fracture with articular step-off or instability
metadata.action: Discuss distal femur ORIF with attending; document vascular exam and articular reduction goals

metadata.trigger: Distal femur fracture with abnormal ankle-brachial index, expanding hematoma, or ischemic signs
metadata.action: Activate vascular surgery consultation and expedite imaging before definitive fixation per attending
